body:
Pat,

Quick follow-up on our call. I've been keeping to the routine — pill first thing, nothing else until I've waited. Most mornings that's fine.

Monday is the snag. Staff meeting at 7:15, so I'm moving fast from the minute I'm up. I pushed my alarm back to make room, but it means the coffee waits. Half an hour of front-desk cheerfulness without coffee is its own kind of test.

Worth it if it's working. I think it is. Will know more at the next check-in.

Phil

Phil,

Sounds like you've got it sorted. The window is thirty to sixty minutes — if you're hitting thirty on the rushed mornings, you're still doing the job right.

The coffee delay is annoying, I know. Plain water is fine right away if that helps at all. Cold comfort, maybe literally.

Keep me posted after the check-in. And if Elaine ever moves that meeting any earlier, I am fully available to write her a strongly worded note on your behalf.

Pat

Hi Pat,

Tried the Land O'Lakes like you suggested. You were right — it doesn't separate. That alone puts it ahead of whatever I'd been using.

Still on the same Folgers medium roast. Elaine keeps a pot going at the inn and I end up with a second cup there on Monday mornings before the 7:15 staff meeting. I've done that long enough that it feels structural at this point.

Anyway — the half-and-half is a yes. Small victory for a Tuesday.

Phil

Phil,

Glad the Land O'Lakes worked out. Consistency is underrated, especially before 7 AM.

Monday 7:15 is not a civilized hour for a staff meeting, for what it's worth. Kevin says the same about his Tuesday calls and his start at 8:30. I have no sympathy for him.

Is the Folgers from the grocery store or did you ever try that smaller place on Elliot Street? Brian mentioned it to me a while back and I keep meaning to ask you.

Pat

Pat,

Folgers from the grocery store. That other place is fine but I'm not paying the difference to feel like I made an effort.

Brian mentioned the coffee shop to you and not once to me. Typical. He really is his father's son in that specific way.

Tell Kevin the 8:30 meetings build character. That's what I would tell someone if I believed it.

Phil

Pat,

The system went down again Monday morning, right in the middle of check-ins. Four rooms queued, PMS froze solid. I ended up doing paper check-ins, which I haven't done since 2011. Progress.

I submitted the error log through the portal but never heard back. Ticket number is BVI-04417. Just want to confirm it's being looked at before the weekend — we're near full and I'd rather not run Elaine through why I'm writing room numbers on a notepad.

Phil

Hi Phil,

Got it — I pulled BVI-04417. The freeze is a session timeout bug in the latest update. Affects a small number of properties. The patch went out Tuesday evening.

Can you confirm the system is behaving normally now? If you see the freeze again before the patch takes hold, a hard refresh (Ctrl+F5) clears it temporarily. Not elegant, but it works.

Sorry for the slow response on the ticket. We've been running behind this week.

Pat
Support Team

Pat,

Checked this morning. Stable so far. I'll keep an eye on it through the weekend.

One more thing — the rate override on Room 214 is still showing the old corporate rate. Guest checks out Friday. I'd rather fix it now than explain a billing discrepancy at 6 AM with a line behind them. Ticket is BVI-04431 if you can get to it.

Thanks.
Phil

Hi Phil,

Room 214 is corrected — the rate will show properly at checkout. You're all set there.

And noted on the session issue. If it freezes again before Friday, message me directly and I'll escalate to the dev team same day. You shouldn't have to fall back on paper twice in one week.

Have a good weekend.

Pat
Support Team

Pat,

Meeting ran long. Elaine added the summer linen rotation to the agenda at the last minute — no prep time, no warning. We got through it.

The good news: staggered check-out reminders finally made the whiteboard. I've been floating that idea for two years. Elaine writing it down is practically a standing ovation from her.

Had to cover the desk solo after because Marcus was late coming in. Got back to my coffee around eight-thirty. Cold. Mondays.

Phil

Phil,

Two years is right. I'd say don't hold your breath, but apparently you don't have to anymore. Small victories.

Cold coffee after a long meeting and a solo desk stretch is genuinely cruel. I hope at least the check-ins were quiet.

Kevin is already asking about Thanksgiving. He started in May. I need someone reasonable to talk to before I agree to something I'll regret — call me when you have ten minutes.

Pat

Hi Pat,

Following up on the ticket from Tuesday. Still getting a 403 error when the booking confirmation script tries to authenticate. It worked once Thursday morning, then quit. Nothing changed on my end.

The account in question is phillane@birchwoodvalleyinn.com. Elaine wants the automated confirmations running before the weekend — full house coming in starting Friday.

Let me know what you need from me.

Phil Lane
Front Desk Manager, Birchwood Valley Inn
Brattleboro, VT

Hi Phil,

Thanks for the follow-up. A 403 on the Gmail API almost always means the OAuth token expired or the scope wasn't saved when access was first granted. Here's what to check:

- Google Workspace Admin > Security > API Controls > Domain-wide delegation
- Confirm the scope https://mail.google.com/ is listed for the service account
- If the token was issued before that scope was added, it won't be valid — you'll need to revoke and re-grant

Try revoking access for phillane@birchwoodvalleyinn.com and re-authorizing through the OAuth consent flow. That clears it about 90% of the time. Reply back with what you see and I'll dig deeper if it doesn't.

Pat
Support Team

Pat,

That fixed it. Revoked, re-granted, ran the script — four confirmation emails went out without a complaint.

I had to rope in my son Brian to navigate the admin panel. Told him it was work-related, which is true enough.

Elaine is happy, the guests are confirmed, and I can stop staring at error logs. Thank you.

Phil Lane
Birchwood Valley Inn

Pat,

Following up on our call last week about the Google Calendar API integration. Still getting a 403 error every time the sync tries to pull from the inn's shared calendar. I cleared the credentials and re-authorized the service account — same result.

What we're trying to do: push the front desk staff schedule at Birchwood Valley Inn automatically to Google Calendar so Elaine isn't updating two separate places every week. Small inn, Brattleboro, nothing exotic. Just want the calendar to reflect reality.

Can you check whether the service account still has the correct scope permissions on your end? I can pull the error logs if that helps move things along.

Thanks,
Phil Lane
Front Desk Manager, Birchwood Valley Inn

Phil,

Thanks for the follow-up. I found it — the calendar.events scope was dropped during a credentials refresh last Tuesday on our side. I've re-added it and rotated the OAuth token.

Please try re-running the sync now. If the 403 is still showing, the next likely cause is the shared calendar's own sharing settings. The service account would need at least "Make changes to events" access granted from within Google Calendar itself. That would fall to whoever manages the inn's Google Workspace account — sounds like possibly Elaine.

Let me know what you see after the next attempt. Ticket stays open.

Pat
Support Team

Pat,

Good news: 403 is gone. Less good news: the events are syncing four or five hours off. Everything is landing in UTC instead of Eastern. It caused a small mess at Monday's 7:15 staff meeting when Elaine pulled up the calendar and none of the shifts matched the printed schedule. I'll say this — it got everyone's attention.

I've looked through the API settings on our end and don't see a timezone field I can set from here. Is that something adjustable on the service account configuration, or does it need to be passed as a property on each individual event in the request?

Appreciate the patience. This is not my natural habitat.

Phil

Phil,

The timezone lives on the event object itself, not the service account. You need timeZone: "America/New_York" inside both the start and end objects of each event request. Without it, the API defaults to UTC.

If your integration is a third-party connector rather than direct API calls, there's almost always a timezone dropdown somewhere in its settings. Either way, happy to walk through it — just let me know what tool or script is making the requests and I can point you to the exact field.

The 403 fix is confirmed closed on my end. I'll leave the timezone thread open until the shifts are landing correctly.

Pat
Support Team

Hi Pat,

Following up on the Google Drive API issue from last week. The shared folder the inn uses for shift reports still isn't syncing correctly — Elaine flagged it again this morning at the 7:15 meeting, so it's officially on my plate now.

From what you walked me through, I think the problem is the service account permissions. I granted the account editor access to the folder, but uploads from the front desk terminal still return a 403. I may have missed a step. I'm not exactly Google's target audience for this sort of thing.

Can you take another look when you have a moment? No urgency on your end — just want it sorted before the weekend rush.

Thanks,
Phil

Hi Phil,

Got it — the 403 is almost certainly a domain delegation gap, not the folder permissions. The service account needs to be granted domain-wide delegation in the Google Workspace admin console, and the OAuth scope for Drive has to be added there too. Editor access on the folder alone won't cover it.

Here's what to pass along to whoever manages the Birchwood Valley Inn's Workspace account:

- Go to Admin Console → Security → API Controls → Domain-wide Delegation
- Add the service account client ID
- Add scope: https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive

If Elaine's the admin, she can do it in about two minutes. If not, you'll need to track down whoever holds the super admin role.

Let me know what you find and I'll stay close to the thread until it's resolved.

Pat

Pat,

Following up on the Obsidian API question from last week. I got the plugin installed but the API calls still aren't pulling through. It just returns a blank pane — no error, nothing.

I'm trying to use it to keep guest notes synced across the front desk tablets here at Birchwood. Elaine approved the idea, so I'd like to get it working before I bring it to the Monday 7:15 meeting. That gives me until tomorrow morning.

If you need the error log I can pull it tonight. Let me know what you need from me.

Phil

Phil,

Thanks for the follow-up. Blank pane with no error usually means the API key isn't being passed in the request header — the plugin silently fails instead of throwing. Easy fix once you know where to look.

Go to Settings → Community Plugins → find the API connector → paste your key into the Bearer Token field. Make sure there's no trailing space (I've wasted an embarrassing amount of time on that one). Save, then restart the plugin, not the whole app.

If that doesn't clear it, send me the log from the developer console (Ctrl+Shift+I, Console tab) and I'll take a look. You've got time before your 7:15.

Pat

Pat,

Trailing space. Of course it was a trailing space.

Fixed, restarted, and it's pulling guest notes clean across all three tablets. Took about four minutes, which is four minutes fewer than I spent staring at a blank pane last Thursday.

I'll put it on the agenda for tomorrow's meeting as a done item rather than a question. Much easier that way. Thank you — genuinely. I'll call you this weekend.

Phil
Front Desk, Birchwood Valley Inn

Hi Pat,

Just wanted to follow up on what we talked about last week. I've been consistent — alarm goes off at 6:00, pill before anything else. The drip coffee waits at least thirty minutes after.

Monday mornings are the tricky ones. Staff meeting at the inn starts at 7:15, so I'm out the door early. I've managed not to skip it, but the timing is tight.

No issues so far. Feeling about the same.

Phil

Hi Phil,

Good to hear you're keeping up with it. Thirty minutes before coffee is the right call — some people stretch it to an hour, but thirty is the standard and it sounds like it's working.

The Monday crunch is worth watching. If 7:15 is pulling you out before the window's up, a second alarm might help — something to remind you not to pour the coffee too soon rather than relying on memory at six in the morning.

How are you feeling overall? Energy, sleep, anything that seems off?

Talk soon,
Pat

Pat,

Second alarm is a good idea. I'll set one for 6:35.

Energy's been okay. Sleep is the same as always — decent but not great, which I've decided is just what this age looks like. Nothing that stands out as different.

Colleen called Sunday and asked if I was taking better care of myself. I told her I was doing fine. Technically accurate.

Phil

Phil,

"Technically accurate" — Dennis would have approved of that answer.

The 6:35 alarm sounds right. Give it two or three weeks and it'll be automatic. One thing worth noting: if the fatigue ever feels heavier than your usual baseline, or anything else shifts, bring it up at your next appointment rather than waiting. Small changes are easier to address early.

You're doing well. Keep the routine going.

Pat

Hi Pat,

Following up on my question from last week about the half-and-half. The Birchwood kitchen goes through roughly two quarts a week with the drip setup, and I want to make sure the recurring order was actually submitted before Friday. Last time it slipped through a gap somewhere and I found out at six-fifty on a Monday morning.

If it did go through, just a confirmation number is fine. If it didn't, let me know what you need from my end to get it moving.

Thanks,
Phil

Hi Phil,

Order is confirmed — two quarts of half-and-half, weekly recurring, first delivery this Friday before noon. Confirmation number is BVI-20251227-441.

I flagged it for morning-window delivery so it's in the kitchen well before your Monday seven-fifteen. You won't be the one rationing cream before a staff meeting ever again, at least not on my watch.

Let me know if anything else comes up.

Pat

Hi Pat,

Talked to Elaine this morning, right after the 7:15 meeting. She was decent about it — agreed to adjust the Tuesday rotation so I'm not pulling a double into Wednesday again. Small victory.

The desk has been busy. Spring bookings picked up faster than last year and we're short a part-timer, so it's been a stretch. Nothing I can't manage, but I wanted you to know why I've been slow returning calls.

Coffee's the only thing keeping me upright, for the record.

Phil

Hi Phil,

Good — glad Elaine listened. I was a little worried you'd let it go like you did back in November.

Are you actually getting days off? And don't say "I'm fine" because you always say that regardless of what's true.

Thinking of you. Call me when you surface.

Pat

Pat,

I am fine. But yes — I have Sunday off and I intend to do very little with it.

Colleen called last night, which helped. Brian sent a text, so progress on that front. The desk should settle now that Elaine posted the new schedule through July. Steadier stretch ahead.

I'll call Sunday. Save me a little of that patience.

Phil

Pat,

Meeting ran long — we didn't wrap until nearly 8:45. Main items: the weekend incident log, Elaine's new breakfast buffer timing starting July, and a first pass at the revised check-in script. Elaine wants front desk sign-off before that last one goes anywhere.

Three things need to come from your end before Thursday: the updated room block spreadsheet, the June maintenance request log, and confirmation from HR on the summer headcount. Let me know if any of those are stuck.

Arrived at 7:15 sharp. Coffee wore off by 8:00.

Phil

Phil,

On it. Room block spreadsheet I can have to you by end of day Tuesday. The June maintenance log is already uploaded to the shared drive — I relabeled the folder so it should actually be findable this time. I'll follow up with HR today about the headcount and copy you on the reply.

One question: is the check-in script revision still in first draft, or did Elaine bring a version to the meeting? Just want to know how much runway we have before she expects notes back.

Pat

Pat,

Still first draft. She handed out a printed copy at the meeting — two pages, landscape, very optimistic tone for something guests read at 6 AM. I've got notes. I'll scan and send tonight.

Unrelated: Brian's birthday is the 12th. Colleen is putting something together and I assume you're already in the loop, but she forgets to call me until Thursday so I thought I'd mention it.

Phil

Phil,

Scanned copy came through — I've added my notes in the margins and I'll email it back Wednesday morning. Agreed on the tone. No one checking in before breakfast needs to be told their stay will be magical.

And yes, Colleen called. Kevin and I are coming. I've got the cake covered. You just have to show up, which I know is your preferred role in these things.

Pat

Pat,

Quick follow-up on our call. I've been keeping to the routine — pill first thing, nothing else until I've waited. Most mornings that's fine.

Monday is the snag. Staff meeting at 7:15, so I'm moving fast from the minute I'm up. I pushed my alarm back to make room, but it means the coffee waits. Half an hour of front-desk cheerfulness without coffee is its own kind of test.

Worth it if it's working. I think it is. Will know more at the next check-in.

The coffee delay is annoying, I know. Plain water is fine right away if that helps at all. Cold comfort, maybe literally.

Keep me posted after the check-in. And if Elaine ever moves that meeting any earlier, I am fully available to write her a strongly worded note on your behalf.

Pat ·0003

Tell Kevin the 8:30 meetings build character. That's what I would tell someone if I believed it.

Phil ·0004

Can you take another look when you have a moment? No urgency on your end — just want it sorted before the weekend rush.

Thanks,

If you need the error log I can pull it tonight. Let me know what you need from me.

Phil ·0020

I flagged it for morning-window delivery so it's in the kitchen well before your Monday seven-fifteen. You won't be the one rationing cream before a staff meeting ever again, at least not on my watch.

Let me know if anything else comes up.

Pat ·0028

Pat ·0047

Hi Phil,

Thanks for the follow-up. A 403 on the Gmail API almost always means the OAuth token expired or the scope wasn't saved when access was first granted. Here's what to check:

- Google Workspace Admin > Security > API Controls > Domain-wide delegation
- Confirm the scope https://mail.google.com/ is listed for the service account
- If the token was issued before that scope was added, it won't be valid — you'll need to revoke and re-grant

Try revoking access for phillane@birchwoodvalleyinn.com and re-authorizing through the OAuth consent flow. That clears it about 90% of the time. Reply back with what you see and I'll dig deeper if it doesn't.

Support Team ·0049

Hi Pat,

Following up on the Google Drive API issue from last week. The shared folder the inn uses for shift reports still isn't syncing correctly — Elaine flagged it again this morning at the 7:15 meeting, so it's officially on my plate now.

Can you take another look when you have a moment? No urgency on your end — just want it sorted before the weekend rush.

Phil ·0054

Hi Phil,

Got it — the 403 is almost certainly a domain delegation gap, not the folder permissions. The service account needs to be granted domain-wide delegation in the Google Workspace admin console, and the OAuth scope for Drive has to be added there too. Editor access on the folder alone won't cover it.

Here's what to pass along to whoever manages the Birchwood Valley Inn's Workspace account:

- Go to Admin Console → Security → API Controls → Domain-wide Delegation
- Add the service account client ID
- Add scope: https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive

If Elaine's the admin, she can do it in about two minutes. If not, you'll need to track down whoever holds the super admin role.

Let me know what you find and I'll stay close to the thread until it's resolved.

Pat,

Phil ·0056

Phil,

Thanks for the follow-up. Blank pane with no error usually means the API key isn't being passed in the request header — the plugin silently fails instead of throwing. Easy fix once you know where to look.

Go to Settings → Community Plugins → find the API connector → paste your key into the Bearer Token field. Make sure there's no trailing space (I've wasted an embarrassing amount of time on that one). Save, then restart the plugin, not the whole app.

If that doesn't clear it, send me the log from the developer console (Ctrl+Shift+I, Console tab) and I'll take a look. You've got time before your 7:15.

Pat ·0057

Pat,

Trailing space. Of course it was a trailing space.

Hi Phil,

Got it — the 403 is almost certainly a domain delegation gap, not the folder permissions. The service account needs to be granted domain-wide delegation in the Google Workspace admin console, and the OAuth scope for Drive has to be added there too. Editor access on the folder alone won't cover it.

Here's what to pass along to whoever manages the Birchwood Valley Inn's Workspace account:

- Go to Admin Console → Security → API Controls → Domain-wide Delegation
- Add the service account client ID
- Add scope: https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive

If Elaine's the admin, she can do it in about two minutes. If not, you'll need to track down whoever holds the super admin role.

Let me know what you find and I'll stay close to the thread until it's resolved.

Phil ·0128

Phil,

Thanks for the follow-up. Blank pane with no error usually means the API key isn't being passed in the request header — the plugin silently fails instead of throwing. Easy fix once you know where to look.

Go to Settings → Community Plugins → find the API connector → paste your key into the Bearer Token field. Make sure there's no trailing space (I've wasted an embarrassing amount of time on that one). Save, then restart the plugin, not the whole app.

If that doesn't clear it, send me the log from the developer console (Ctrl+Shift+I, Console tab) and I'll take a look. You've got time before your 7:15.

Pat ·0129

Pat,

Trailing space. Of course it was a trailing space.

I'll put it on the agenda for tomorrow's meeting as a done item rather than a question. Much easier that way. Thank you — genuinely. I'll call you this weekend.
